H400.kjv_def: eating, food, meal(-time), meat, prey, victuals.

H401.derivation: or אֻכָּל; apparently from H398 (אָכַל); devoured;

H402.usage: meat(8x), devour(3x), fuel(3x), eat(2x), consume(1x), food(1x)

H403.kjv_def: but, certainly, nevertheless, surely, truly, verily.
H403.usage: surely(9x), but(3x), verily(2x), truly(2x), certainly(1x), nevertheless(1x)

H404.derivation: a primitive root; apparently meaning to curve (as with a burden);

H405.strongs_def: a load; by implication, a stroke (others dignity)

H406.derivation: from an unused root meaning to dig;

H408.strongs_def: not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative); once (Job 24:25) as a noun, nothing
H408.kjv_def: nay, neither, [phrase] never, no, nor, not, nothing (worth), rather than.
H408.usage: never(2x), nay(1x), neither(1x), no(1x), none(1x), nor(1x), not(1x), nothing(1x), whither(1x)

H409.strongs_def: {not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative); once (Job 24:25) as a noun, nothing}

H410.strongs_def: strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty (but used also of any deity)
H410.kjv_def: God (god), [idiom] goodly, [idiom] great, idol, might(-y one), power, strong. Compare names in '-el.'
H410.usage: God(213x), god(16x), power(4x), mighty(5x), goodly(1x), great(1x), idols(1x), might(1x), strong(1x)

H411.derivation: a demonstrative particle (but only in a plural sense)

H413.derivation: (but only used in the shortened constructive form אֶל ); a primitive particle; properly, denoting motion towards, but occasionally used of a quiescent position, i.e.
H413.strongs_def: near, with or among; often in general, to
H413.kjv_def: about, according to, after, against, among, as for, at, because(-fore, -side), both...and, by, concerning, for, from, [idiom] hath, in(-to), near, (out) of, over, through, to(-ward), under, unto, upon, whether, with(-in).

H414.derivation: a variation of H424 (אֵלָה)lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה; oak;

H415.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H430 (אֱלֹהִים) and H3478 (יִשְׂרָאֵל); the mighty god if Jisrael;
H415.strongs_def: El-Elohi-Jisrael, the title given to a consecrated spot by Jacob

H416.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H1008 (בֵּית־אֵל); the God of Bethel;
H416.strongs_def: El-Bethel, the title given to a consecrated spot by Jacob

H419.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H1730 (דּוֹד); God has loved;

H420.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H3045 (יָדַע); God of knowledge;

H421.derivation: a primitive root (rather identical with H422 (אָלָה) through the idea of invocation);

H422.strongs_def: properly, to adjure, i.e. (usually in a bad sense) imprecate

H424.strongs_def: an oak or other strong tree

H425.derivation: the same as H424 (אֵלָה)lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה;
H425.strongs_def: Elah, the name of an Edomite, of four Israelites, and also of a place in Palestine
H425.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H427.derivation: A variation of H424 (אֵלָה)lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה

H428.kjv_def: an-(the) other; one sort, so, some, such, them, these (same), they, this, those, thus, which, who(-m).

H430.strongs_def: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative
H430.kjv_def: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.
H430.usage: god(244x), judge(5x), GOD(1x), goddess(2x), great(2x), mighty(2x), angels(1x), exceeding(1x), godly(1x)

H433.derivation: rarely (shortened) אֱלֹהַּ; probably prolonged (emphatic) from H410 (אֵל);

H436.strongs_def: an oak or other strong tree

H438.strongs_def: Allon, an Israelite, also a place in Palestine
H438.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H439.derivation: from H437 (אַלּוֹן) and a variation of H1068 (בְּכִית); oak of weeping;

H440.derivation: or rather (shortened) אֵלֹנִי; patron from H438 (אַלּוֹן);
H440.strongs_def: an Elonite or descendant (collectively) of Elon

H441.strongs_def: familiar; a friend, also gentle; hence, a bullock (as being tame; applied, although masculine, to a cow); and so, a chieftain (as notable, like neat cattle)
H441.kjv_def: captain, duke, (chief) friend, governor, guide, ox.
H441.usage: duke(57x), guide(4x), friends(2x), governors(2x), captains(1x), governor(1x), ox(2x)

H442.strongs_def: Alush, a place in the Desert

H443.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H2064 (זָבַד); God has bestowed;
H443.strongs_def: Elzabad, the name of two Israelites

H444.strongs_def: to muddle, i.e. (figuratively and intransitive) to turn (morally) corrupt

H445.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H2603 (חָנַן); God (is) gracious;

H446.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H1 (אָב); God of (his) father;
H446.strongs_def: Eliab, the name of six Israelites

H447.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) repeated; God of (his) God;
H447.strongs_def: Eliel, the name of nine Israelites

H448.derivation: or (contraction) אֱלִיָּתָה; from H410 (אֵל) and H225 (אוּת); God of (his) consent;

H449.derivation: from the same as H419 (אֶלְדָּד); God of (his) love;

H450.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H3045 (יָדַע); God (is) knowing;
H450.strongs_def: Eljada, the name of two Israelites and of an Aramaean leader

H451.derivation: from H422 (אָלָה) (in the original sense of strength);
H451.strongs_def: the stout part, i.e. the fat tail of the Oriental sheep

H452.derivation: or prolonged אֵלִיָּהוּ; from H410 (אֵל) and H3050 (יָהּ); God of Jehovah;
H452.strongs_def: Elijah, the name of the famous prophet and of two other Israelites

H453.derivation: or (fully) אֱלִיהוּא; from H410 (אֵל) and H1931 (הוּא); God of him;
H453.strongs_def: Elihu, the name of one of Job's friends, and of three Israelites

H454.derivation: or (shortened) אֶלְיוֹעֵינַי; from H413 (אֵל) and H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H5869 (עַיִן); towards Jehovah (are) my eyes;
H454.strongs_def: Eljehoenai or Eljoenai, the name of seven Israelites

H455.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H2244 (חָבָא); God will hide;

H456.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H2779 (חֹרֶף); God of autumn;

H457.strongs_def: good for nothing, by anal. vain or vanity; specifically an idol
H457.kjv_def: idol, no value, thing of nought.

H458.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); God of (the) king;

H459.derivation: (Aramaic) or shorter אִלֵּן; prolonged from H412 (אֵל);

H460.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H3254 (יָסַף); God (is) gatherer;
H460.strongs_def: Eljasaph, the name of two Israelites

H461.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5828 (עֵזֶר); God of help;
H461.strongs_def: Eliezer, the name of a Damascene and of ten Israelites

H463.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5971 (עַם); God of (the) people;

H464.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6337 (פָּז); God of gold;
H464.strongs_def: Eliphaz, the name of one of Job's friends, and of a son of Esau

H465.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6419 (פָּלַל); God of judgment;

H466.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6395 (פָּלָה); God of his distinction;

H467.derivation: or (shortened) אֱלְפֶּלֶט; from H410 (אֵל) and H6405 (פַּלֵּט); God of deliverance;
H467.strongs_def: Eliphelet or Elpelet, the name of six Israelites

H468.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6697 (צוּר); God of (the) rock;

H469.derivation: or (shortened) אֶלְצָפָן; from H410 (אֵל) and H6845 (צָפַן); God of treasure;

H470.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6958 (קוֹא); God of rejection;

H471.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H6965 (קוּם); God of raising;
H471.strongs_def: Eljakim, the name of four Israelites

H472.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H7651 (שֶׁבַע) (in the sense of H7650 (שָׁבַע)); God of (the) oath;

H474.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H7769 (שׁוּעַ); God of supplication (or of riches);
H474.strongs_def: Elishua, the son of King David

H475.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H7725 (שׁוּב); God will restore;
H475.strongs_def: Eljashib, the name of six Israelites

H476.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H8085 (שָׁמַע); God of hearing;
H476.strongs_def: Elishama, the name of seven Israelites

H478.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H8199 (שָׁפַט); God of judgment;

H481.strongs_def: to tie fast; hence (of the mouth) to be tongue-tied
H481.kjv_def: bind, be dumb, put to silence.

H484.derivation: probably of foreign derivation (used thus only in the plural);

H485.derivation: or (masculine) אָלֻם; passive participle of H481 (אָלַם);

H487.derivation: from H427 (אַלָּה) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); oak of (the) king;

H488.derivation: prolonged from H481 (אָלַם) in the sense of bereavement;

H490.strongs_def: a widow; also a desolate place

H492.derivation: from H489 (אַלְמֹן) in the sense of concealment;
H492.strongs_def: some one (i.e. so and so, without giving the name of the person or place)

H493.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5276 (נָעֵם); God (is his) delight;

H494.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5414 (נָתַן); God (is the) giver;
H494.strongs_def: Elnathan, the name of four Israelites

H495.strongs_def: Ellasar, an early country of Asia

H496.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5749 (עוּד); God has testified;

H497.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5710 (עָדָה); God has decked;

H498.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5756 (עוּז) (in the sense of H5797 (עֹז)); God (is) defensive;

H499.derivation: from H410 (אֵל) and H5826 (עָזַר); God (is) helper;
H499.strongs_def: Elazar, the name of seven Israelites
